---
name: megaplan
description: Run an idea through the megaplan planning loop with enforced sequencing and audit artifacts.
---

# Megaplan

Use this skill when the user wants a plan to go through a structured planner and critic loop before execution.

You must route every workflow step through `megaplan`. Do not call `claude` or `codex` directly for the workflow itself, because that bypasses enforcement and audit logging.

## Start

Run:

```bash
megaplan init --project-dir "$PROJECT_DIR" "$IDEA"
```

Read the JSON response and report:
- The plan name
- The current state
- The next step

## Workflow

Always follow the `next_step` or `valid_next` fields returned by the CLI.

Standard loop:
1. `megaplan plan`
2. `megaplan critique`
3. `megaplan evaluate`
4. If recommendation is `CONTINUE`, run `megaplan integrate` and loop back to critique.
5. If recommendation is `SKIP`, run `megaplan gate`.
6. If recommendation is `ESCALATE` or `ABORT`, present the result (including `suggested_override` and `override_rationale`) and use `megaplan override ...` only if the user chooses to bypass.
7. After a successful gate, run `megaplan execute --confirm-destructive`.
8. Finish with `megaplan review`.

If the user says "run autonomously" or similar, proceed through steps without
waiting for confirmation at each checkpoint. Still report results at the end.

## Session Defaults

Both agents default to **persistent** sessions. The Codex critic carries context
across iterations so it can verify its own prior feedback was addressed.

Override flags (mutually exclusive):
- `--fresh` — start a new persistent session (break continuity)
- `--ephemeral` — truly one-off call with no session saved
- `--persist` — explicit persistent (default; mainly for Claude review override)

## Reporting

After each step, summarize:
- What happened from `summary`
- Which artifacts were written
- What the next step is

If the user wants detail, inspect the artifact files under `.megaplan/plans/<plan-name>/`.

## Useful Commands

```bash
megaplan status --plan <name>
megaplan audit --plan <name>
megaplan list
megaplan override add-note --plan <name> "user context"
megaplan override abort --plan <name>
```

alter table public.timelines
  add column if not exists config_version integer not null default 1;

create or replace function public.update_timeline_config_versioned(
  p_timeline_id uuid,
  p_expected_version integer,
  p_config jsonb
)
returns table (config_version integer)
language sql
security invoker
set search_path = public
as $$
  update public.timelines
  set
    config = p_config,
    config_version = timelines.config_version + 1,
    updated_at = timezone('utc', now())
  where timelines.id = p_timeline_id
    and timelines.config_version = p_expected_version
  returning timelines.config_version;
$$;

grant execute on function public.update_timeline_config_versioned(uuid, integer, jsonb) to authenticated;
grant execute on function public.update_timeline_config_versioned(uuid, integer, jsonb) to service_role;

`1` and created `public.update_timeline_config_versioned(...)` as the explicit optimistic-locking write path. Verification: the function is defined as `returns table (config_version integer)` over a conditional `UPDATE ... WHERE id = p_timeline_id AND config_version = p_expected_version RETURNING timelines.config_version`, which yields zero rows on version mismatch; `git diff --check` passes for the migration file.","files_changed":["supabase/migrations/20260326100000_add_timeline_config_version.sql"],"commands_run":["sed -n '1,240p' supabase/migrations/20260325090000_create_video_editor_tables.sql","sed -n '1,220p' supabase/migrations/20260326100000_add_timeline_config_version.sql","rg -n \"config_version|update_timeline_config_versioned\" supabase/migrations/20260326100000_add_timeline_config_version.sql","git diff --check -- supabase/migrations/20260326100000_add_timeline_config_version.sql"]},{"task_id":"T3","status":"done","executor_notes":"Created `public.timeline_agent_sessions` with the requested columns, `(timeline_id, status)` index, RLS enabled, and user-scoped select/insert/update policies. Verification: the migration includes the `auth.uid() = user_id` policy checks and a guarded `alter publication supabase_realtime add table public.timeline_agent_sessions`; `git diff --check` passes for the migration file.","files_changed":["supabase/migrations/20260326100001_create_timeline_agent_sessions.sql"],"commands_run":["rg -n \"timeline_agent_sessions|supabase_realtime|alter publication\" supabase/migrations | sed -n '1,240p'","sed -n '1,240p' supabase/migrations/20260326100001_create_timeline_agent_sessions.sql","git diff --check -- supabase/migrations/20260326100001_create_timeline_agent_sessions.sql"]},{"task_id":"T4","status":"done","executor_notes":"Added `AgentSessionStatus`, `AgentTurn`, and `AgentSession` in `src/tools/video-editor/types/agent-session.ts`, matching the session table columns and nullable `summary` field. Verification: compared the new type file against the T3 schema and ran targeted ESLint successfully on the new TypeScript surface.","files_changed":["src/tools/video-editor/types/agent-session.ts"],"commands_run":["sed -n '1,120p' src/tools/video-editor/types/agent-session.ts","sed -n '1,240p' supabase/migrations/20260326100001_create_timeline_agent_sessions.sql","npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/types/agent-session.ts --ext ts --max-warnings 0"]},{"task_id":"T5","status":"done","executor_notes":"Extracted the Groq singleton and Whisper transcription call into `supabase/functions/_shared/transcription.ts`, then refactored `ai-voice-prompt` to import and use them. Verification: the helper preserves `model: \"whisper-large-v3-turbo\"`, `temperature: 0`, and `response_format: \"verbose_json\"`; the transcribe-only branch still returns the raw `transcription`; targeted ESLint and `git diff --check` both pass.","files_changed":["supabase/functions/_shared/transcription.ts","supabase/functions/ai-voice-prompt/index.ts"],"commands_run":["sed -n '1,220p' supabase/functions/ai-voice-prompt/index.ts","sed -n '1,220p' supabase/functions/_shared/transcription.ts","npx eslint supabase/functions/ai-voice-prompt/index.ts supabase/functions/_shared/transcription.ts --ext ts --max-warnings 0","git diff --check -- supabase/functions/_shared/transcription.ts supabase/functions/ai-voice-prompt/index.ts"]},{"task_id":"T11","status":"done","executor_notes":"Added `useAgentVoice` as a thin wrapper over `useVoiceRecording` with `task: \"transcribe_only\"`, forwarding only `result.transcription` to the provided `onTranscription` callback and exposing the requested recording state/actions. Verification: inspected `useVoiceRecording`'s callback contract, confirmed the wrapper ignores `prompt`, and ran targeted ESLint plus `git diff --check` successfully.","files_changed":["src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentVoice.ts"],"commands_run":["sed -n '1,240p' src/shared/hooks/useVoiceRecording.ts","sed -n '1,220p' src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentVoice.ts","rg -n \"useAgentVoice|useVoiceRecording\\(|transcribe_only|task:\\s*'transcribe_only'|task:\\s*\\\"transcribe_only\\\"\" src | sed -n '1,240p'","npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentVoice.ts --ext ts --max-warnings 0","git diff --check -- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentVoice.ts"]}],"sense_check_acknowledgments":[{"sense_check_id":"SC1","executor_note":"The migration adds `config_version` idempotently and defines the RPC as a conditional `UPDATE ... RETURNING config_version` table function. By inspection, a wrong `p_expected_version` produces zero updated rows and therefore an empty RPC result; I did not execute the migration against a database in this batch."},{"sense_check_id":"SC3","executor_note":"The sessions migration enables RLS, creates select/insert/update policies scoped by `auth.uid() = user_id`, and guards publication enrollment with a `pg_publication_tables` check before `alter publication supabase_realtime add table public.timeline_agent_sessions`."},{"sense_check_id":"SC4","executor_note":"The new TypeScript types line up with the T3 schema: string ids/timestamps, `status` narrowed to the six DB enum-like values, `turns` as `AgentTurn[]`, `model` as required string, and `summary` as nullable."},{"sense_check_id":"SC5","executor_note":"The refactor keeps the same Groq Whisper model and params in `transcribeAudio`, and `ai-voice-prompt` still uses the returned text for both the transcribe-only early return and the downstream prompt-writing flow. Targeted ESLint passed with the new shared import."},{"sense_check_id":"SC11","executor_note":"The wrapper hook passes `task: \"transcribe_only\"` into `useVoiceRecording` and calls `onTranscription(transcription)` from `onResult`, so only the raw transcription is forwarded and the optional enhanced prompt is ignored."}]}

items.text: Inspect relevant files and megaplan execution expectations for batch 1 tasks
Implement migrations, shared transcription extraction, and agent session/voice types/hooks
Run targeted verification, checkpoint batch progress, and summarize results

item.text: Batch 2 covers the optimistic-locking UI plumbing, the first `ai-timeline-agent` server pieces, and the client session hooks. I’m reading the existing data provider, edge bootstrap helpers, and task/session APIs first so the new agent path matches the repo’s current contracts instead of inventing parallel ones.

items.text: Inspect the existing timeline save flow, edge function helpers, and task/session client patterns relevant to T2/T6/T7/T8/T10
Implement T2 UI config_version propagation and T6/T7/T8/T10 agent/server/client scaffolding
Run targeted verification, write batch 2 checkpoint, and return structured task updates

item.text: I’ve mapped the main seams now. Next I’m reading the actual `create-task` contract, the editor query keys/runtime, and a couple of edge-function examples so the new agent session hooks and scaffold align with the existing auth, Supabase, and React Query patterns.

  TimelinePlaybackContextValue,
  UseTimelineStateResult,
} from '@/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.types';
import { useTimelineTrackManagement } from '@/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement';

export type { SaveStatus, RenderStatus, EditorPreferences } from '@/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData';

export function useTimelineState(): UseTimelineStateResult {
  const runtime = useVideoEditorRuntime();
  const playback = useTimelinePlayback();
  const dataHook = useTimelineData();
  const { selectedProjectId } = useProjectSelectionContext();

  const dragCoordinator = useDragCoordinator({
    dataRef: dataHook.dataRef,
    scale: dataHook.scale,
    scaleWidth: dataHook.scaleWidth,
    startLeft: TIMELINE_START_LEFT,
    rowHeight: ROW_HEIGHT,
  });

  const assetManagement = useAssetManagement({
    dataRef: dataHook.dataRef,
    selectedTrackId: dataHook.selectedTrackId,
    selectedProjectId,
    setSelectedClipId: dataHook.setSelectedClipId,
    setSelectedTrackId: dataHook.setSelectedTrackId,
    applyTimelineEdit: dataHook.applyTimelineEdit,
    patchRegistry: dataHook.patchRegistry,
    registerAsset: dataHook.registerAsset,
    uploadAsset: dataHook.uploadAsset,
    invalidateAssetRegistry: dataHook.invalidateAssetRegistry,
    resolveAssetUrl: runtime.provider.resolveAssetUrl.bind(runtime.provider),
  });

  const externalDrop = useExternalDrop({
    dataRef: dataHook.dataRef,
    scale: dataHook.scale,
    scaleWidth: dataHook.scaleWidth,
    selectedTrackId: dataHook.selectedTrackId,
    applyTimelineEdit: dataHook.applyTimelineEdit,
    patchRegistry: dataHook.patchRegistry,
    registerAsset: dataHook.registerAsset,
    uploadAsset: dataHook.uploadAsset,
    invalidateAssetRegistry: dataHook.invalidateAssetRegistry,
    resolveAssetUrl: runtime.provider.resolveAssetUrl.bind(runtime.provider),
    coordinator: dragCoordinator.coordinator,
    registerGenerationAsset: assetManagement.registerGenerationAsset,
    uploadImageGeneration: assetManagement.uploadImageGeneration,
    handleAssetDrop: assetManagement.handleAssetDrop,
  });

  const clipResize = useClipResize({
    dataRef: dataHook.dataRef,
    applyTimelineEdit: dataHook.applyTimelineEdit,
  });

  const clipEditing = useClipEditing({
    dataRef: dataHook.dataRef,
    resolvedConfig: dataHook.resolvedConfig,
    selectedClipId: dataHook.selectedClipId,
    selectedTrack: dataHook.selectedTrack,
    currentTime: playback.currentTime,
    setSelectedClipId: dataHook.setSelectedClipId,
    setSelectedTrackId: dataHook.setSelectedTrackId,
    applyTimelineEdit: dataHook.applyTimelineEdit,
    applyResolvedConfigEdit: dataHook.applyResolvedConfigEdit,
  });

  const trackManagement = useTimelineTrackManagement({
    dataRef: dataHook.dataRef,
    resolvedConfig: dataHook.resolvedConfig,
    selectedClipId: dataHook.selectedClipId,
    setSelectedTrackId: dataHook.setSelectedTrackId,
    applyTimelineEdit: dataHook.applyTimelineEdit,
    applyResolvedConfigEdit: dataHook.applyResolvedConfigEdit,
  });

  const editor = useMemo<TimelineEditorContextValue>(() => ({
    data: dataHook.data,
    resolvedConfig: dataHook.resolvedConfig,
    selectedClipId: dataHook.selectedClipId,
    selectedTrackId: dataHook.selectedTrackId,
    selectedClip: dataHook.selectedClip,
    selectedTrack: dataHook.selectedTrack,
    selectedClipHasPredecessor: dataHook.selectedClipHasPredecessor,
    compositionSize: dataHook.compositionSize,
    trackScaleMap: dataHook.trackScaleMap,
    scale: dataHook.scale,
    scaleWidth: dataHook.scaleWidth,
    isLoading: dataHook.isLoading,
    dataRef: dataHook.dataRef,
    coordinator: dragCoordinator.coordinator,
    indicatorRef: dragCoordinator.indicatorRef,
    editAreaRef: dragCoordinator.editAreaRef,
    preferences: dataHook.preferences,
    timelineRef: playback.timelineRef,
    timelineWrapperRef: playback.timelineWrapperRef,
    setSelectedClipId: dataHook.setSelectedClipId,
    setSelectedTrackId: dataHook.setSelectedTrackId,
    setActiveClipTab: dataHook.setActiveClipTab,
    setAssetPanelState: dataHook.setAssetPanelState,
    registerGenerationAsset: assetManagement.registerGenerationAsset,
    onCursorDrag: playback.onCursorDrag,
    onClickTimeArea: playback.onClickTimeArea,
    onActionResizeStart: clipResize.onActionResizeStart,
    onActionResizeEnd: clipResize.onActionResizeEnd,
    onOverlayChange: clipEditing.onOverlayChange,
    onTimelineDragOver: externalDrop.onTimelineDragOver,
    onTimelineDragLeave: externalDrop.onTimelineDragLeave,
    onTimelineDrop: externalDrop.onTimelineDrop,
    handleAssetDrop: assetManagement.handleAssetDrop,
    handleDeleteClip: clipEditing.handleDeleteClip,
    handleSelectedClipChange: clipEditing.handleSelectedClipChange,
    handleResetClipPosition: clipEditing.handleResetClipPosition,
    handleSplitSelectedClip: clipEditing.handleSplitSelectedClip,
    handleSplitClipAtTime: clipEditing.handleSplitClipAtTime,
    handleToggleMute: clipEditing.handleToggleMute,
    handleTrackPopoverChange: trackManagement.handleTrackPopoverChange,
    handleReorderTrack: trackManagement.handleReorderTrack,
    handleRemoveTrack: trackManagement.handleRemoveTrack,
    moveSelectedClipToTrack: trackManagement.moveSelectedClipToTrack,
    moveClipToRow: trackManagement.moveClipToRow,
    createTrackAndMoveClip: trackManagement.createTrackAndMoveClip,
    uploadFiles: dataHook.uploadFiles,
  }), [
    dataHook.compositionSize,
    dataHook.data,
    dataHook.dataRef,
    dataHook.isLoading,
    dataHook.preferences,
    dataHook.resolvedConfig,
    dataHook.scale,
    dataHook.scaleWidth,
    dataHook.selectedClip,
    dataHook.selectedClipHasPredecessor,
    dataHook.selectedClipId,
    dataHook.selectedTrack,
    dataHook.selectedTrackId,
    dataHook.setActiveClipTab,
    dataHook.setAssetPanelState,
    dataHook.setSelectedClipId,
    dataHook.setSelectedTrackId,
    dataHook.trackScaleMap,
    dataHook.uploadFiles,
    assetManagement.handleAssetDrop,
    assetManagement.registerGenerationAsset,
    clipEditing.handleDeleteClip,
    clipEditing.handleResetClipPosition,
    clipEditing.handleSelectedClipChange,
    clipEditing.handleSplitSelectedClip,
    clipEditing.handleSplitClipAtTime,
    clipEditing.handleToggleMute,
    clipEditing.onOverlayChange,
    clipResize.onActionResizeEnd,
    clipResize.onActionResizeStart,
    dragCoordinator.coordinator,
    dragCoordinator.editAreaRef,
    dragCoordinator.indicatorRef,
    externalDrop.onTimelineDragLeave,
    externalDrop.onTimelineDragOver,
    externalDrop.onTimelineDrop,
    playback.onClickTimeArea,
    playback.onCursorDrag,
    playback.timelineRef,
    playback.timelineWrapperRef,
    trackManagement.createTrackAndMoveClip,
    trackManagement.handleRemoveTrack,
    trackManagement.handleReorderTrack,
    trackManagement.handleTrackPopoverChange,
    trackManagement.moveClipToRow,
    trackManagement.moveSelectedClipToTrack,
  ]);

  const chrome = useMemo<TimelineChromeContextValue>(() => ({
    timelineName: runtime.timelineName ?? null,
    saveStatus: dataHook.saveStatus,
    renderStatus: dataHook.renderStatus,
    renderLog: dataHook.renderLog,
    renderDirty: dataHook.renderDirty,
    renderProgress: dataHook.renderProgress,
    renderResultUrl: dataHook.renderResultUrl,
    renderResultFilename: dataHook.renderResultFilename,
    setScaleWidth: dataHook.setScaleWidth,
    handleAddTrack: trackManagement.handleAddTrack,
    handleClearUnusedTracks: trackManagement.handleClearUnusedTracks,
    unusedTrackCount: trackManagement.unusedTrackCount,
    handleAddText: clipEditing.handleAddText,
    reloadFromServer: dataHook.reloadFromServer,
    startRender: dataHook.startRender,
  }), [
    runtime.timelineName,
    dataHook.reloadFromServer,
    dataHook.renderDirty,
    dataHook.renderLog,
    dataHook.renderProgress,
    dataHook.renderResultFilename,
    dataHook.renderResultUrl,
    dataHook.renderStatus,
    dataHook.saveStatus,
    dataHook.setScaleWidth,
    dataHook.startRender,
    clipEditing.handleAddText,
    trackManagement.handleAddTrack,
    trackManagement.handleClearUnusedTracks,
    trackManagement.unusedTrackCount,
  ]);

  const playbackValue = useMemo<TimelinePlaybackContextValue>(() => ({
    currentTime: playback.currentTime,
    previewRef: playback.previewRef,
    playerContainerRef: playback.playerContainerRef,
    onPreviewTimeUpdate: playback.onPreviewTimeUpdate,
    formatTime: playback.formatTime,
  }), [
    playback.currentTime,
    playback.formatTime,
    playback.onPreviewTimeUpdate,
    playback.playerContainerRef,
    playback.previewRef,
  ]);

  return {
    editor,
    chrome,
    playback: playbackValue,
  };
}

item.text: The remaining uncertainty is mostly around typing and conflict UX. I’m editing the save-flow files and adding the first agent/session files together so the new RPC, session table, and direct realtime subscription all line up in one pass instead of leaving half-integrated types behind.

I’ve finished the read pass. The save flow needs one extra touch beyond the task list: the existing conflict dialog only opens from the realtime hook, so I’m wiring `saveStatus='error'` into that same dialog rather than inventing a second conflict UI path.

The first implementation pass is in. I’m checking types now because this batch crosses browser React Query code, the typed data provider, and Deno edge code at once; the likely fixes are around query return shapes and a few strict-TypeScript edges in the new hooks.

Splitting file placement without fixing typing and queue semantics would just move debt around.  \nExecution notes: sequence inside the batch matters. First make queue scheduling idempotent. Second introduce typed registry/accessors. Third pull runtime modules under one package boundary. Last, thin `toolSettingsService` into a facade over smaller modules. This is the cluster most likely to prevent repeated future settings issues.\n\nCluster \"lora-import-cycle-break\"  \nScope: `src/domains/lora/components/index.ts`, modal subtree under `src/domains/lora/components/LoraSelectorModal/**`, direct `HuggingFaceTokenSetup` imports.  \nWhy this is one batch: both findings are the same production cycle reported from two dimensions.  \nExecution notes: tiny, low-risk batch. Keep one explicit external-entrypoint check before deleting no-caller shims.\n\nCluster \"supabase-facade-burn-down\"  \nScope: `eslint.supabase-facade-allowlist.js`, direct `@/integrations/supabase/client` import sites in active subsystems.  \nWhy this is one batch: this is a governed migration with clear backlog pressure. It should be handled as a tracked burn-down rather than folded into unrelated feature work.  \nExecution notes: choose one subsystem slice, reduce the allowlist, and add a dated budget target. This is a recurring `incomplete_migration` and `cross_module_architecture` root cause.\n\nCluster \"image-generation-ownership-and-coverage\"  \nScope: `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/**`, `src/shared/components/modals/ImageGenerationModal.tsx`, owning tool/feature boundaries for image generation.  \nWhy this is one batch: the architecture and testing issues are the same problem from two angles. The first deliverable should be a real component-level integration test that locks current behavior before boundary movement.\n\nCluster \"media-gallery-association-logic\"  \nScope: `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useShotPositionChecks.ts`, `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useShotPositioning.ts`.  \nWhy this is one batch: one real predicate duplication issue remains after the broader \u201cworkflow hub\u201d concern was downgraded.  \nExecution notes: keep this narrow. Finally, move legacy travel settings handling to a boundary adapter so the controller/tests target the canonical shape.\n\nCluster \"generation-mutation-contract-cleanup\"  \nScope: `src/domains/generation/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`.  \nWhy this is one batch: only one genuine issue survived here, and it is localized ceremony. item.text: Batch 3 is the first end-to-end slice: the real tool loop on the edge side and the actual chat panel components on the client. I’m reading the current agent scaffold, the Groq call patterns already in the repo, and a few reusable UI primitives before I patch so the loop and chat UI fit the existing code instead of bolting on a parallel stack.

items.text: Inspect current ai-timeline-agent scaffold, Groq usage patterns, and reusable UI primitives needed for T9/T12
Implement T9 tool-use loop plus prompt template and build T12 chat panel components
Run targeted verification, checkpoint batch 3, and return structured task updates

item.text: Batch 3 is focused on wiring the agent loop and the chat panel. I’m checking the existing UI primitives and the current editor data shape before patching `ai-timeline-agent` and the new `AgentChat` components.

The editor already has the primitives needed for a compact sidebar UI. I’m reading the timeline types and the current agent scaffold now so the tool loop can be added without guessing at the config shape or the Groq response format.

The timeline types are straightforward and the current scaffold is still a thin persistence wrapper. I’ve confirmed the agent loop needs to own loading `config` and `asset_registry` itself, and the chat UI can stay self-contained on top of the existing session hooks.

{"output":"Completed batch 4 tasks T13 and T14.
